---
title: Building a Static Site Generator in Rust
author: Jane Doe
date: 2025-04-21
---
# Building a Static Site Generator in Rust

[[TOC]]

## Introduction

Static site generators (SSGs) have become the **go-to solution** for building fast, secure websites. In this post, we'll walk through building one in [Rust](https://rust-lang.org).

> "Simplicity is the ultimate sophistication." — Leonardo da Vinci

## Prerequisites

Before we begin, make sure you have:

1. Rust 1.80+ installed (`rustup update stable`)
2. A text editor (VS Code, Neovim, etc.)
3. Basic familiarity with HTML and Markdown

## Project Structure

```
my-ssg/
├── Cargo.toml
├── src/
│   ├── main.rs
│   └── lib.rs
├── content/
│   └── posts/
│       └── hello-world.md
└── templates/
    └── base.html
```

## The Core: Markdown to HTML

The heart of any SSG is converting Markdown to HTML. Here's our core function:

```rust
use html_generator::{generate_html, HtmlConfig};

fn convert(markdown: &str) -> String {
    let config = HtmlConfig {
        add_aria_attributes: true,
        generate_toc: true,
        minify_output: true,
        ..HtmlConfig::default()
    };
    generate_html(markdown, &config).unwrap()
}
```

### Front Matter Parsing

Every blog post starts with metadata:

```yaml
---
title: My First Post
date: 2025-01-15
tags: [rust, web]
---
```

### Handling Code Blocks

We support syntax highlighting for multiple languages:

| Language | Extension | Highlighting |
|----------|-----------|-------------|
| Rust | `.rs` | ✅ |
| Python | `.py` | ✅ |
| JavaScript | `.js` | ✅ |
| HTML | `.html` | ✅ |
| CSS | `.css` | ✅ |

## Accessibility Matters

Our generator automatically adds ARIA attributes:

- `<nav>` gets `role="navigation"` and `aria-label`
- `<button>` gets `aria-label` derived from content
- `<form>` gets `aria-labelledby` with generated IDs
- `<input type="checkbox">` gets associated `<label>` elements

:::warning
Always test your site with a screen reader before deploying!
:::

## Performance Benchmarks

We tested with a 500-page site:

- **Build time**: 1.2 seconds ⚡
- **Output size**: 2.3 MB (minified)
- **Lighthouse score**: 💯

## Conclusion

Building an SSG in Rust gives you:

- [x] Blazing fast builds
- [x] Memory safety
- [x] Great error messages
- [ ] A social life (working on it 😄)

---
